title: A Year Later, Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill Act Has a Clean Tech Silver Lining
summary: Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act gutted the wind and solar credits at the heart of Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act. But a year on, the law’s preserved incentives for geothermal, advanced nuclear, and grid-scale storage may do more to cut global emissions than the credits it eliminated.

title: The China challenge

title: Ex-Olympian charged over alleged reflecting pool damage
summary: The Trump administration has indicted a former Olympian over damage to the National Mall's reflecting pool. A lawyer for David Hearn says he's innocent.

summary: Democrats are hoping they can win back the House majority this fall, but fighting within the party could complicate their plans.

title: Extreme heat advisory delays National Mall July 4 event, organizers say

title: Cruise ship docked in San Francisco hit by norovirus outbreak
summary: A Princess Cruises cruise ship docked in San Francisco has seen an outbreak of norovirus, according to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The CDC said that 102 out of the over 3,000 passengers onboard the Ruby Princess have been affected, along with 23 crewmembers.

title: Marriage Penalties and Bonuses in the Federal Tax Code
summary: When a couple marries, their federal income tax payments are likely to be impacted. Marriage may cause a couple’s tax payments to increase, resulting in a marriage tax penalty, or decrease, resulting in a marriage tax bonus. Marriage penalties and bonuses emerge because of the impossibility of balancing three competing goals in the income tax code: progressivity (higher-income taxpayers pay higher marginal tax...

summary: In a 6-3 decision on June 29, 2026, the Supreme Court held that statutory provisions that allow the President to remove Federal Trade Commission (FTC) commissioners only “for cause” violate the Constitution. title: Indra Nooyi Says India's Chaos Is The Cost Of Democracy Compared To China's Model

summary: Hoover Institution director Condoleezza Rice recently sat down with former PepsiCo CEO Indra Nooyi about Indra's candid description of India as a “chaotic country”.

title: Maine Senate Race Is Tight, With Concerns About Both Candidates
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Daron Shaw said, "Along with North Carolina, Maine is the Democrats’ most obvious Senate target... How do you blow that? Maybe by nominating someone facing allegations of racism and dishonesty. This race could demonstrate whether partisan and populist loyalties trump all else."

title: Teacher Pension Problems Continue To Get Bigger
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Joshua Rauh said, “When you hear framings like ‘schools are underfunded’ or ‘schools are overfunded,’ usually those are missing the point. It’s that a growing share of what we already spend never reaches students, and until that problem is directly confronted, even the largest tax increases will struggle to deliver on their stated promises.”

title: The Continental Congress wrote the Declaration. Is its modern descendent living up?
summary: The Second Continental Congress produced the Declaration of Independence 250 years ago. Its legacy can also be seen in the Congress we know today. But is the legislative branch living up to the aspirations of that era?

title: Grand jury indicts Louisiana's attorney general in fight over changes to local courts
summary: Louisiana's Republican attorney general was indicted Thursday on criminal charges by a grand jury in New Orleans, accused of trying to intimidate local officials who fought a law enacted by GOP legislators to overhaul the local courts.

title: How legal battles across the country could complicate the midterms
summary: As President Trump attempts to wield greater power over election administration ahead of the midterms, dozens of legal battles are currently underway across the country. Their outcomes could shape how elections are run this fall. Liz Landers discussed more with Ben Ginsberg. He has spent decades working as a Republican election lawyer, including on the landmark 2000 Bush v. Gore dispute.

title: Haberman and Swan on 'Regime Change,' their book on Trump's unconstrained 2nd term
summary: A new book by veteran reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan is offering one of the most revealing looks to date inside the second Trump White House. It's called "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump." Amna Nawaz spoke with Haberman and Swan about why those close to the president were willing to speak, and what those sources wanted readers to understand.

title: Europe's leaders close ranks as they face barbs and pressure from Trump
summary: After Trump questioned Italy's reliability as a wartime ally and claimed Giorgia Meloni had groveled for his attention, European leaders rallied to Meloni's side. It is the latest example of how the often divisive American president is helping to draw Europe closer together.

title: Farm Bill Primer: Programs Without a Budget Baseline
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) provides budgetary projections of government spending called baselines. title: How Schools and After-School Programs Can Collaborate to Support Academic Alignment
summary: The authors provide national data about the state of academic alignment between school-day and after-school learning, illustrate how schools and after-school programs put academic alignment into practice, and identify enabling and impeding factors.

title: Military Effectiveness Should Be Determined by The War Department Not Judges

title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.
